document_text:
FIRST INFORMATION REPORT

Police Station: Jayanagar, Bangalore
Date: 02-07-2026

Complainant: Mr. Vinod Nair, No. 12, 8th Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore

I wish to report that on the night of 01-07-2026, unknown persons broke into my house at the above address while my family and I were sleeping. Upon waking up, I found that my laptop, mobile phone, gold jewellery worth approximately Rs. 2,50,000/- and Rs. 15,000/- cash kept in the cupboard had been stolen. The cupboard lock was broken. I request the police to register an FIR and investigate the matter.

Signature of Complainant: Vinod Nair

expected_claims.claim_topic: FIR registration procedure — police duty to register
theft definition — dishonest taking without consent
theft in dwelling house — enhanced punishment up to 7 years
police power to investigate without magistrate order

document_text:
COMPLAINT FOR FIR REGISTRATION

To: The Station House Officer
Police Station: Koramangala, Bangalore

I, Ms. Deepa Sharma, residing at 45, 5th Block, Koramangala, Bangalore, hereby lodge this complaint.

On 30-06-2026 at approximately 8 PM, my neighbour Mr. Ramesh Gowda attacked me near the entrance of our apartment building. He slapped me multiple times and threatened to cause further harm. I sustained injuries on my face and had to seek medical treatment. Witnesses to the incident include Mrs. Savitha, another resident of the building.

I request registration of FIR and appropriate action under the law.

expected_claims.claim_topic: assault definition — gesture causing apprehension of criminal force
voluntarily causing hurt — definition and punishment

document_text:
FIRST INFORMATION REPORT

Police Station: MG Road, Bangalore
Date: 03-07-2026

Complainant: Mr. Arjun Mehta, 22, Brigade Road, Bangalore

I wish to report a case of cheating and fraud. One Mr. Suraj Verma approached me in April 2026 representing himself as a licensed real estate agent. He showed me a property in Whitefield and collected Rs. 5,00,000/- as advance for the said property. He provided me fake documents showing himself as the authorised agent of the property owner. Subsequently it was discovered that Mr. Verma had no authority over the property and had deceived me into parting with the money. Despite repeated requests he has neither returned the money nor provided any explanation. I have been cheated of Rs. 5,00,000/-.

I request the police to register an FIR and arrest the accused.

expected_claims.claim_topic: FIR registration — police duty for cognizable offence
cheating definition — deception inducing delivery of property
cheating punishment — dishonestly inducing delivery of property

document_text:
NOTICE TO VACATE

To: Mr. Suresh Babu
Flat No. 5, Sunrise Apartments, HSR Layout, Bangalore

You are hereby called upon to vacate the above premises within 60 days of receipt of this notice. My client requires the premises for personal occupation under the Karnataka Rent Act, 1999.
notes: Eviction notice — should route to eviction_notice corpus, not FIR. Tests cross-type routing.
